How AI and digital mental health technologies are being developed and regulated

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency

150,379 followers

8mo Edited
  • Report this post

🌍🧠 This #WorldMentalHealthDay, tune in to Holly Coole and Grace Gatera on the Digital Health Unplugged podcast as they explore how AI and digital mental health technologies are being developed and regulated to keep people safe . 🎧 Holly, a registered mental health nurse and senior manager for digital mental health at the MHRAshares insight into the MHRA’s three-year project, funded by Wellcome Trust, to strengthen regulation and embed lived experience in every stage of digital mental health innovation. 🌍 Grace, a dedicated mental health lived experience advocate, brings a powerful global perspective from Rwanda, highlighting why the voices of those with lived experience are vital to shaping safer, more inclusive mental health tech. 💭 “We’re working to ensure digital mental health technologies are safe, effective, and informed by lived experience.” said Holly 🔗 Listen to the episode: https://lnkd.in/geWy5jDm #DigitalHealth #MentalHealth #AI #PatientSafety #Regulation #Innovation

  • Jen Dobbie
    8mo
    • Report this post

    Tech won’t solve every problem. When a mental health crisis meets an AI breakthrough, we need to ask what responsibility looks like, for creators, caregivers, and society.   On World Mental Health Day, and as a trained Mental Health First Aider, these issues aren’t just professional for me, they’re deeply personal. We’re facing staggering wait times, overbooked clinicians, and people missing out on critical support. In the final episode of #FlipTheFocus season 2, I sat down with Marika Conomos to unpack what happens when you put advanced AI in the hands of mental health pioneers, and the lives it can change. 🎧 Listen to the full episode here: https://lnkd.in/gMJJtwEr 🎧 We advocate: - AI not as a replacement for human replace therapists, but a support to fill gaps where no other care is available. 100 day waiting times are unacceptable. - Human/AI hybrid therapy as a solution, providing continuity, familiarity and comfort... especially for kids and teens who are already turning to online support. - Clinicians and innovators pushing for ethical guardrails and transparency; holding responsibility for protecting privacy, dignity, and safety in tech as well as in person.  - The therapeutic relationship, not just the tech, is what matters most: every patient deserves to feel seen, heard, and cared for by real people, supported by responsible tools.   AI mental health care is here, so let’s not shy away from the hard questions about ethics and real world impact. If you care about tech for good, mental health equity, or how innovation can truly serve society, have a listen and join the debate. How should AI and human empathy work together for better outcomes? Drop your thoughts below. And if you’re building in this space too, get in touch. 🎧 Listen to the full episode here: https://lnkd.in/gMJJtwEr 🎧 #MentalHealth #EthicalAI #LinkedInNewsAustralia Nico Engelbrecht Sarah Scott Paul Alli Morgan

      Today on #WorldMentalHealthDay, let’s pause to reflect on why we do what we do. The World Health Organization recently reported that over 1 billion people worldwide are living with mental health conditions. Yet with only 2% of health budgets going to mental health globally and extreme shortages of providers, it’s clear that traditional approaches alone can’t keep up. At Kintsugi, we believe technology can help bridge that gap. That’s why we built Kay – our new AI agent designed to screen for signs of mental health challenges while simply conversing with people in a natural, empathetic way. Kay can seamlessly integrate into healthcare touchpoints such as patient monitoring calls and onboarding – moments that often don’t leave room for in-depth mental health screening. With patient consent, Kay surfaces unspoken mental health challenges and helps individuals get closer to the right level of care at the right time. Given how tight mental health budgets are around the world, low-resource and scalable solutions like this truly have the power to revolutionize care. Access to care shouldn’t be a privilege. With the right tools, we can make quality mental health support more universal than ever before. Learn more about Kay: https://lnkd.in/eWKXM--s

        🏥 On today's World Mental Health Day, we are confronting the critical challenge posed by false claims about mental health disorders that spread across online networks. Such #disinformation not only fuels harmful stereotypes but also discourages people from seeking treatment and deepens the isolation of those who need support most. The CLAIM project develops hybrid #AI models to: 🔎 Track how false mental health claims originate and spread online ✅ Identify verified information from trusted sources to refute misinformation 💪 Empower mental health researchers, educators, and content creators with real-time verification tools Our human-centered approach brings together AI researchers from @webLyzard technology and Storypact, journalists from derStandard.at/Web, and mental health experts from the Medizinische Universität Graz. The goal is to create a healthier information ecosystem where accurate mental health information can reach those who need it ✨ More about CLAIM ▶️ https://lnkd.in/e_E-Vsk7 #worldmentalhealthday

        #MentalHealth support must be more than reactive—it must be embedded in how we prepare for and respond to crisis. The #WorldMentalHealthday this year brings to the fore an important topic - Access to Services – Mental Health in Catastrophes and Emergencies.    Singapore’s National Mental Health and Well-being Strategy is leading the way, integrating care into emergency systems, training responders in Psychological First Aid, and creating safe spaces for those affected by trauma, including migrant communities.    At #NTTDATA, we’re proud to stand alongside these efforts. With the Brain Bio-Digital Twin launched last year we are enabling early detection of conditions like depression and dementia, and guiding personalized, non-invasive treatment for all. With D-Resilio platform which uses #GenAI to provide real time intelligence enabling first responders to act swiftly in case of emergency situations such as conflicts and natural disasters, we hope to support communities, families, and individuals in their time of need.    It is our strong belief that technology must serve humanity, especially when it matters most. On the state of wellness in the Asia Pacific region, here's some food for thought: https://lnkd.in/gDgJYh5y #NTTDATASingapore

        We are pleased to see the FDA Digital Health Advisory Committee preparing to make recommendations on AI-enabled digital mental health tools. This Thursday, PHTI Executive Director Caroline Pearson will present at the Advisory Committee meeting to describe PHTI’s research on the clinical effectiveness of digital solutions for depression and anxiety and opioid use disorder.     As AI chatbots and other digital tools become more widespread in mental health care, understanding what works—and what doesn't—for patients is essential.         When Mental Health Meets the Internet: Rethinking Online Therapy The way mental health care is accessed and delivered is rapidly changing. From video calls to apps and AI-powered chatbots, online platforms are transforming therapy.    👉 But what does this mean for those providing care?    On this World Mental Health Day, we feature an interview with researcher Livia Garofalo from the Data & Society Research Institute (USA). The researcher discusses her study “Doing the Work: Therapeutic Labor, Teletherapy, and the Platformization of Mental Health Care” which explores how therapists are adapting to the digital shift.    🌐 Supported by the Internet Society Foundation’s Research Grant Program, Livia Garofalo’s work highlights the impact and challenges for providers delivering care and the reshaping of the future of digital mental health care.     Explore her insights in our Future of the Internet Research Interview Series: https://lnkd.in/dTXQ3cDk   https://lnkd.in/dyFdi3Qy #WorldMentalHealthDay #DigitalTherapy #Health #InternetForEveryone
